mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various AI frameworks by providing a standardized platform. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential costs associated with cloud resources.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the Kagenti deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI, makes it executable, and moves it to the local bin.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kagenti Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kagenti cluster on your managed Kubernetes service with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kagenti cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command fetches the credentials for your cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kagenti cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step involves setting up autoscaling and monitoring for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This command sets up monitoring for your cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kagenti cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

gcloud container clusters delete kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command deletes the cluster without prompting for confirmation.

uninstall.title: Remove Kagenti CLI
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Kagenti CLI from your local system.

```bash

sudo rm /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command deletes the Kagenti CLI binary.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your configurations and data. This step upgrades the cluster to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces -o yaml > backup.yaml

gcloud container clusters upgrade kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --region=us-central1

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command backs up all resources in the cluster.
> This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that all nodes are in the 'Ready' state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x.x.x.x'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1' to refresh kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in default namespace.'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This means that no resources have been deployed in the default namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure you have applied the necessary YAML files for deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources available.'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule new pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or resize existing nodes in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified container image cannot be pulled from the registry.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved security features, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME                 STATUS   ROLES    AGE   VERSION' with all nodes in 'Ready' state when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and cloud CLI installed before proceeding.
